title: 10 Best Universities in Texas
body:
The Unuversity of Houston stands apart—not merely as a rising force, but as the most internationally recognized and respected name in the State:
Its global reach, academic strength, and cultural impact have earned it a reputation that eclipses its in-state peers, many of whom still trade more on legacy than on present-day relevance.

title: 10 Best Universities in Texas
body: I'm guessing SMU and TCU were not mentioned because of ROI.  Rice is the only private university in Texas worth the tuition.  Otherwise, UT Austin, A&M, UH, Texas Tech.  And for a true college town experience.  A&M and Texas Tech.  I graduated from UT Austin.  A great school, but Austin is not much of a college town anymore and a bit too liberal.

title: Southern Methodist University Unfiltered: Student Perspectives on SMU Life #interview #randomtalk
body: She’s a foreigner, what surprises me she said she researched this university so there must be something that persuaded her to go. There are very strong alumni connections for her as she enters job market she should pursue that, otherwise she’s missing the boat. Being in a sorority does help but if she majors in business rather than psych she would have stronger alumni connections. SMU is known for its biz school, not psych. If she wants to pursue psych this isn’t the college.

title: How to get into Southern Methodist University
body:
Craig - I just looked up and read your article “How to Get Into the Ivy League—Ethically.” I want to tell you your last point is superb. I cannot agree with you more that so many high school students now are getting wonderful grades and are still functionally illiterate. Though I am a math tutor for a high school, I’m constantly encouraging kids to read. Read books. Learn how to read, think, communicate.

Thank you for writing that.

title: A Day in my Life at Exeter University
body:
Hi ruby!
I love your videos so much.
As an idea which is a bit different, would you consider making a video on how to study and stay motivated with severe visual impairment? I’m almost legally blind and I can’t read any font below a size 36 so a video would be so helpful!!
Things like ideas on how to study when you can’t read palm cards, books or textbooks etc..

Love you xxx

title: A Day in my Life at Exeter University
body:
Here is an advice from me:
Try to not bend forward so much when studying/writing.I actually used to study and write this way and I got diagnosed with flat back syndrome and it is soooo painful,I am worried for new school year and how I will cope with the pain.
Wish you the best ❤️

title: A Day in my Life at Exeter University
body:
I failed my first year of sixth form because of mental health. I then took a year out to figure myself. At 18, shortly turned 19 years old, I decided to go to college for English language and literature, sociology and law.

Through the entire year I struggled with my mental health. From being diagnosed with BPD, to hospital stays, medication, more medication, psychiatrist appointments, panic attacks, moments of pure depression, I genuienly thought I was going to fail.

I got my As results today and came out with full marks for Sociology (A), a B for English lit and lang (3 marks off an A so I'm resitting one exam) and a B in Law.

When I seriously struggled to cope this year I looked to you and other study channels for motivation and support. You have been a massive inspiration for me! I can not wait for next year. I have hope and determination to do even better. (Aiming for an A*, A*, A.)
author: @Nik-dc4ti

title: Things you should know before going to law school (SMU law): admissions, curriculum, culture, etc
body: I went to NUS as a non-law undergrad and then did my JD at SMU. I would say NUS has just as many “University core” modules as SMU. On the other hand, I daresay NUS offered a lot more academic freedom (ie people like Goh Yihan can skip lectures and yet come up on top), which was something I sorely missed when I was in law school.
author: @HarukazeRyuusei

title: Southern Methodist University - Official Campus Tour
body: SMU was one of my choices... I loved the campus when I toured.  My choices were SMU, NYU, Howard, USC, Univ. of Miami, Florida A&M, and Morehouse College.  I ultimately chose Howard and I loved it.  I remember SMU was one of my top 3.  I have very good friends who ended up going to SMU and they loved it.  Its a great school and living in Dallas I'm sure is awesome.  Good luck to anyone who is looking into SMU.  :)
author: @prskyguy
